(a) A justice of the peace or municipal court judge may not issue an order related to a title except as provided by Chapter 47 (Disposition of Stolen Property) , Code of Criminal Procedure, or Section 27.031 (Jurisdiction) (a)(3), Government Code.

(b) A county or district court judge may not order the department to change the type of title for:

(1) a nonrepairable vehicle titled after September 1, 2003; or

(2) a vehicle for which the department has issued a certificate of authority under Section 683.054 (Certificate of Authority to Dispose of Vehicle) .
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 501. Certificate of Title Act
##### § 501.0521 — Court Ordered Title Changes

(a) A justice of the peace or municipal court judge may not issue an order related to a title except as provided by Chapter 47 (Disposition of Stolen Property) , Code of Criminal Procedure, or Section 27.031 (Jurisdiction) (a)(3), Government Code.

(b) A county or district court judge may not order the department to change the type of title for:

    (1) a nonrepairable vehicle titled after September 1, 2003; or

    (2) a vehicle for which the department has issued a certificate of authority under Section 683.054 (Certificate of Authority to Dispose of Vehicle) .

*Added by Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1135 (H.B.2741), Sec. 53, eff. September 1, 2013.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A justice of the peace or municipal court judge may not issue an order related to a title except as provided by Chapter 47 (Disposition of Stolen Property) , Code of Criminal Procedure, or Section 27.031 (Jurisdiction) (a)(3), Government Code.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A county or district court judge may not order the department to change the type of title for:
a nonrepairable vehicle titled after September 1, 2003; or
a vehicle for which the department has issued a certificate of authority under Section 683.054 (Certificate of Authority to Dispose of Vehicle) .

history.raw: Added by Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1135 (H.B.2741), Sec. 53, eff. September 1, 2013.
